summary: Kent Walker, President of Global Affairs at Google, and Hartmut Neven, Lead of Google Quantum AI, highlight urgent concerns over digital security as quantum computers progress. Quantum computing threatens current encryption standards, as malicious actors may already be gathering encrypted data for future decryption once a cryptographically relevant quantum computer becomes available. The United States National Institute for Standards and Technology introduced post-quantum cryptography standards in 2024 following years of global collaboration. Google reports ongoing efforts since 2016, pioneering post-quantum cryptography integration and crypto agility, and is on track to migrate within current guidelines. The company urges a society-wide approach and policy action to safeguard critical infrastructure and ensure robust security in anticipation of advancing quantum capabilities.
